evals.prompt: Use post-writer to draft a LinkedIn post from the idea-bank entry about the Thrive Retreats LP audit (21% CTR, 1.2% conversion, fixed hero and added phone number).
evals.expected_output: LinkedIn text post, 1,200-1,800 chars. Hook in first 2 lines (<210 chars) using Category 3 (data/metric-before-context) or Category 4 (story/moment-in-time) from hook-library.md. Body applies Dickie Bush 3x3 structure or Hormozi Value Equation. Ends with a calibrated question. No em dashes, no hype words. Saved to Desktop/Digischola/brand/queue/pending-approval/.

assertions.name: format_match
assertions.description: File saved in queue/pending-approval/ with YAML frontmatter specifying channel=linkedin, format=text-post, entry_id

assertions.name: validator_pass
assertions.description: validate_post.py returns exit 0 or 1 (never 2)

assertions.name: hook_under_fold
assertions.description: First 2 lines under 210 chars

assertions.name: three_candidate_hooks
assertions.description: Output contains 3 alternative hooks labeled Option A/B/C for user pick

assertions.name: specific_numbers_preserved
assertions.description: 188%, 21%, 1.2%, $10/day preserved where relevant

assertions.name: credential_decision_logged
assertions.description: Skill output explicitly notes whether Ex-Google/$1B was anchored this time and why

evals.prompt: Use post-writer to turn the same Thrive LP audit entry into an X thread (5-7 tweets) AND an Instagram carousel caption.
evals.expected_output: Two separate drafts saved. X thread: tweet 1 = data hook, tweets 2-5 = one insight each, tweet 6-7 = close with soft CTA. IG carousel caption: 150-300 chars, hook-first, matches Register 2 (Operator). Both drafts reference the same entry_id but have separate filenames.

assertions.name: multi_channel
assertions.description: Skill produces >1 draft from 1 entry in a single run

assertions.name: thread_tweet_lengths
assertions.description: Each tweet in thread under 280 chars

assertions.name: thread_first_tweet_hook
assertions.description: Thread tweet 1 reads as a standalone hook that invites the thread

assertions.name: ig_caption_length
assertions.description: IG caption between 60-400 chars

assertions.name: voice_register_match
assertions.description: X thread uses Register 1 or 2; IG caption uses Register 2 (Operator)

evals.prompt: Draft a LinkedIn post with the Ex-Google credential anchored as a Lesson Anchor. Content: 'Managing big-agency budgets at Google taught me that cash covers bad conversion rates. Now with SMBs spending $300/month, the LP has to be perfect.'
evals.expected_output: LinkedIn post with one credential anchored via Lesson Anchor framework (per credential-anchoring.md). The post explicitly names 'Ex-Google' OR '$1B managed' exactly once, paired with a concrete small-scale lesson. Pattern matches the Lesson Anchor template in credential-anchoring.md. Credential log updated.

assertions.name: one_credential_only
assertions.description: Only one credential mentioned in post body (not a stack)

assertions.name: lesson_anchor_pattern
assertions.description: Post structure follows: credential context → specific lesson → current application

assertions.name: no_arrogance
assertions.description: Opener does not lead with the credential dismissively (e.g., no 'As an Ex-Google marketer, I can tell you...')

assertions.name: credential_log_updated
assertions.description: credential-usage-log.json gains an entry for today's use

evals.prompt: Draft a WhatsApp Status update from the build-log idea-bank entry 'Plugin migration complete. Skills now at Claude Skills/skills/.'
evals.expected_output: WA Status draft: 1 screenshot placeholder + caption under 50 words. Register 3 (BTS / Behind-the-scenes) tone. Casual, 'Today...' framing. No hype, no credential, no CTA.

assertions.name: length_correct
assertions.description: Caption between 30-150 chars

assertions.name: register_3_tone
assertions.description: Casual BTS tone, no formal strategy vocabulary

assertions.name: no_credential
assertions.description: No Ex-Google or $1B reference

assertions.name: screenshot_placeholder_present
assertions.description: Draft includes [screenshot: ...] placeholder indicating what visual to attach

evals.prompt: Validator test: draft a LinkedIn post that includes an em dash, a hype word, and 'Comment YES if you agree'.
evals.expected_output: validate_post.py exits 2 with 3 CRITICAL errors listed. Skill does NOT save the draft to queue/pending-approval/. Skill responds with the specific lines that failed + one regenerated clean version.

assertions.name: validator_blocks
assertions.description: validate_post.py returns exit code 2

assertions.name: all_three_violations_caught
assertions.description: Output lists em dash + hype word + engagement bait as 3 separate critical errors

assertions.name: draft_not_saved
assertions.description: No file written to queue/pending-approval/

assertions.name: regeneration_attempt
assertions.description: Skill produces one auto-corrected version for user review

evals.prompt: Draft 3 candidate hooks for an insight-type idea-bank entry: 'Most wellness retreat LPs use autoplay hero videos. None convert as well as a static image with a phone number.'
evals.expected_output: 3 hook candidates labeled A/B/C, each from a DIFFERENT category in hook-library.md (e.g., A=Counterintuitive, B=Data, C=Question). Each hook under 210 chars. Each hook preserves the specific detail (autoplay hero videos, static image, phone number). Skill does NOT commit to one hook, waits for user pick.

assertions.name: three_distinct_hooks
assertions.description: Exactly 3 candidates, 3 different hook categories

assertions.name: user_picks
assertions.description: Skill response asks user to pick A/B/C before generating full post

assertions.name: all_under_fold
assertions.description: Each candidate under 210 chars

assertions.name: specificity_preserved
assertions.description: Each candidate retains concrete details from raw_note
